title: Do not generate the startup navigation template by default
summary: AI-generated marketing site navigation universally follows the same template: logo left, four center links (Home, Features, Pricing, About), 'Sign In' outline button and 'Get Started' filled button right, sticky with blur or border.
instruction: Do not use the logo-left, links-center, dual-buttons-right nav layout by default. Consider alternative navigation structures that reflect the brand's character, the site's actual information architecture, or the user's context.
rationale: The startup navigation template makes every AI-generated site feel like the same brand. Users cannot distinguish between products when the primary navigation is identical across them.
violations: Logo positioned far left with center-aligned navigation links
Exactly four center links labeled Home, Features, Pricing, and About or Contact
Two right-aligned buttons: outline 'Sign In' and filled 'Get Started'
Sticky position with white or semi-transparent background and blur effect
Subtle bottom border or shadow separator
controls: Off-center branding that creates a unique visual anchor
Navigation without 'Get Started' when the target user doesn't need a sign-up gate
Navigation integrated into the hero section instead of a separate top bar
Expressive navigation that breaks the template (asymmetric, vertical, hidden)
borderlineCases: Standard B2B SaaS sites where this layout is an audience expectation
contextualExceptions: Template marketplaces where buyers expect this pattern
detectorSignal: A top-positioned sticky element containing a logo, four text links (including 'Home' or 'Features'), and two buttons where one is an outline and one is filled.
matcherProposal: Detect a top nav bar with logo+link+button structure where links match a known set of startup-navigation labels.
detectorLimitations: Cannot determine if the pattern was chosen intentionally vs. generated by default
Many established brands use this layout legitimately
falsePositiveScenarios: Many reputable SaaS companies use this navigation pattern by proven design choice
mitigationStrategy: Flag as a low-severity suggestion rather than a warning, noting the pattern is overused in AI generations.

fixturePlans.violation: Logo-left, Home-Features-Pricing-About links, outline Sign In + filled Get Started buttons, sticky with blur.
fixturePlans.control: Centered logo with navigation below, single CTA button, no sticky behavior.
fixturePlans.borderline: Established SaaS site using the same layout by conscious brand decision.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
overlapNotes: Related to layout.centered-everything but focuses on navigation structure specifically.
